🔬 BioKhoj Help

Personal literature radar — track what matters in biomedical research.

Quick Start

Get Started in 30 Seconds
  1. Open the sidebar — click the BioKhoj icon in your toolbar or press Ctrl+Shift+K
  2. Go to the Watch tab — add a gene (BRCA1), disease (glioblastoma), drug, pathway, or any biomedical concept
  3. Switch to Recent — BioKhoj queries PubMed, bioRxiv, and OpenAlex, then ranks results by signal bars
  4. Browse Hot — see trending preprints across bioRxiv, medRxiv, PubMed, and Europe PMC
The sidebar uses a 4-tab layout: Recent, Watch, Search, and Hot. Tabs are shown at the top of the sidebar. Below 300px width, tab labels hide and only icons are shown.

Sidebar Tabs

Recent (default tab)

Your paper feed. Papers are grouped by date (Today, Yesterday, This Week, Older) and sorted by relevance.

  • Signal bars — 1–4 bars indicate relevance strength based on recency, citations, journal tier, and entity match
  • Unread dots — blue dot on papers you have not yet opened; cleared on click
  • Entity chips — matched watchlist entities shown as colored chips on each paper card (same deterministic color as in Watch tab)
  • Actions — bookmark, open in new tab, or dismiss individual papers

This is the default tab when opening the sidebar normally.

Watch

Manage your tracked entities. Each entity is shown as a colored chip.

  • Add bar — type an entity name and press Enter to add. Auto-classifies the type (gene, disease, drug, pathway, organism, method).
  • Entity chips — colored with deterministic hash-based colors (see Entity Colors below). Click × to remove.
  • Onboarding — when the watchlist is empty, helpful prompts guide you to add your first entities

The sidebar opens directly to this tab when you use "Watch in BioKhoj" from the right-click menu.

Search

Unified database search across multiple biomedical sources in a single query:

  • PubMed — literature search
  • NCBI Gene — gene records
  • ClinVar — clinical variant annotations
  • ClinicalTrials — clinical trial records
  • UniProt — protein entries

Results from all databases appear together, grouped by source. Click any result to open the full record.

Hot

Trending preprints and recent papers from multiple sources:

  • bioRxiv — biology preprints
  • medRxiv — medical preprints
  • PubMed — recently published articles
  • Europe PMC — European life sciences literature

Each source has a collapsible, color-coded header. Expand or collapse individual sources to focus on what you care about. Papers are ranked by attention and recency within each source.

Paper Selection & Cleanup

Managing Your Paper List

The Recent tab supports explicit paper management via select mode:

  1. Enter select mode — click the "Select" button at the top of the Recent tab
  2. Check papers — checkboxes appear next to each paper; click to select individual papers
  3. Delete selected — remove the checked papers from your feed
  4. Clear all — remove all papers at once

Select mode exits automatically after a delete action, or click "Cancel" to exit without changes.

Recently Opened History

Track What You Have Read

At the bottom of the Recent tab, a collapsible "Recently Opened" section shows the last 15 papers you clicked on.

  • Title and time ago — each entry shows the paper title and relative time ("2 min ago", "3 hours ago")
  • Persisted across sessions — history survives browser restarts and sidebar closes
  • Collapsible — click the header to expand or collapse the list

Entity Colors

Deterministic Color Coding

Entity chips use hash-based deterministic colors from an 8-color palette. The same entity always gets the same color everywhere:

  • Watch tab — entity chips in your watchlist
  • Recent tab — matched entity chips on paper cards

Colors are computed from the entity name, so BRCA1 always appears in the same hue regardless of where it is displayed. No configuration needed.

Context Menu Actions

Right-Click Integration

Selected text — select any text on a webpage, then right-click:

  • "Watch in BioKhoj" — adds the selected text as a new watchlist entity (opens Watch tab)
  • "Lookup in BioKhoj" — searches for the selected text across all databases (opens Search tab)

Links to papers — right-click any PubMed, bioRxiv, or DOI link:

  • "Save to BioKhoj reading list" — bookmarks the paper for later reading

These actions work on any webpage — PubMed, journal sites, Wikipedia, lab wikis, or anywhere you encounter a term or paper link.

Keyboard Shortcuts

Navigate Without a Mouse
  • Ctrl+Shift+K — toggle sidebar open/closed
  • 1 2 3 4 — switch to Recent, Watch, Search, Hot tabs
  • j / k — navigate down/up in paper lists
  • Enter — open the selected paper
  • Escape — close overlays or deselect

Smart Tab Defaults

Which Tab Opens When
  • Normal open (toolbar icon or Ctrl+Shift+K) — opens the Recent tab
  • Right-click "Watch in BioKhoj" — opens the Watch tab with the new entity added
  • Right-click "Lookup in BioKhoj" — opens the Search tab with query pre-filled
  • Last tab remembered — if you switch tabs manually, your last active tab is restored on next open

Narrow Width

Responsive Layout

When the sidebar is narrower than 300px, tab labels are hidden and only icons are shown. This keeps the tab bar usable even in very narrow sidebar configurations. All functionality remains the same.

Full App

More Features on the Web

The extension sidebar provides the core experience. For advanced features, open the full BioKhoj app:

Open BioKhoj at lang.bio/biokhoj

The full app includes:

  • Trend charts — publication volume over time for each entity, with sparklines and overlays
  • Reading list — save papers, add notes/tags, track read status, export
  • Author tracking — watch specific authors and get alerts on new publications
  • Concept expansion — suggestions for related entities from OpenAlex and MeSH
  • Export — BibTeX, RIS, Markdown, weekly digest, watchlist JSON backup
  • Journal tiers — customize journal rankings that affect Signal Score

Your watchlist syncs between the extension and PWA via shared local storage.

Privacy

Your Data Stays Local
  • 100% local storage — watchlist, reading list, history, and settings never leave your browser
  • API calls only for papers — queries go directly to PubMed, bioRxiv, NCBI, UniProt, ClinVar, and Europe PMC (no proxy)
  • No analytics — no telemetry, tracking, or usage data collected
  • No account required — works without sign-up or login

About

BioKhoj

BioKhoj — personal literature radar for biomedical research.

Built by Oric Labs. Part of the BioLang ecosystem.

Report issues: GitHub Issues

For full documentation including Signal Score formula, co-mention algorithms, and all settings, see the complete help page.

BioKhoj — 100% client-side. Your data never leaves your browser.
